title: eriogonum kearneyi subsp. monoense
intro: Eriogonum kearneyi subsp. monoense, also known as Mono Buckwheat or Mono Mormon Tea, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, Polygonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments such as sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Eriogonum kearneyi subsp. monoense include its ability to help soothe and calm the body, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which have been used for centuries by indigenous communities in the American West.
